<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******. Wrap>> <<every word bigram>> <<in double angular>> <<brackets, such as>> <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. There>> <<should be 6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and only>> <<paragraphs are separated>> <<with each other by>> <<two new lines as if>> <<it was '\n\n' in>> <<python. Paragraph 5>> <<must start with word>> <<team. Include keyword>> <<possession in your>> <<response.>>  

<<The first function>> <<will recursively>> <<traverse each element>> <<in the list and>> <<check if it is a>> <<tuple. If it is,>> <<it will iterate>> <<through its items,>> <<summing any numbers>> <<and continuing the>> <<process for nested>> <<tuples. Non-numeric>> <<items will be>> <<ignored. This approach>> <<ensures that all>> <<numeric values,>> <<regardless of their>> <<depth in the structure,>> <<are accounted for.>>  

<<The second function>> <<uses a generator>> <<to yield numbers>> <<from the nested>> <<tuples. This method>> <<is memory efficient>> <<as it does not>> <<store all values>> <<at once. It leverages>> <<recursion to explore>> <<each tuple, and>> <<for each item, it>> <<either adds it to>> <<the sum if it is>> <<a number or calls>> <<the function again>> <<if it is a tuple.>>  

<<Both functions>> <<must ensure that>> <<the input is properly>> <<processed. They should>> <<handle any level of>> <<nesting and avoid>> <<errors by checking>> <<the type of each>> <<element. The use of>> <<recursion is key>> <<to navigating the>> <<complex structures. It>> <<also helps in>> <<maintaining the>> <<clarity
